info.title: openconfig-system
info.description:
Model for managing system-wide services and functions on
network devices.

Portions of this code were derived from IETF RFC 7317.
Please reproduce this note if possible.

IETF code is subject to the following copyright and license:
Copyright (c) IETF Trust and the persons identified as authors of
the code.
All rights reserved.

Redistribution and use in source and binary forms, with or without
modification, is permitted pursuant to, and subject to the license
terms contained in, the Simplified BSD License set forth in
Section 4.c of the IETF Trust's Legal Provisions Relating
to IETF Documents (http://trustee.ietf.org/license-info).

get.summary:
The time limit (in seconds) to use when connecting to the directory server.
This is distinct from the time limit specified in timelimit and affects
the setup of the connection only.

get.summary:
Specifies whether Unix Attributes have been enabled for Active Directory users,
which provide the uidNumber and gidNumber attributes from the directory. In a
generic AD environment without additional capabilities installed, these attributes
may need to be derived from the objectSid. This attribute has no effect for non-AD
servers.

get.summary:
Specifies what checks to perform on server certificates in a TLS session.
The <level> can be specified as one of never, allow, try, demand, or hard.
A tls_cacert is required if peer verification is enabled.

depth.name: depth
depth.description: limit the depth of nodes in response

fields.name: fields
fields.description: optionally identify specific data nodes in response

filter.name: filter
filter.description: xpath expression to filter data nodes in response

insert.name: insert
insert.description: controlling the order when adding new list elements

point.name: point
point.description: used to specify the insertion point

accounting-method-id.name: accounting-method-id
accounting-method-id.description:
An ordered list of methods used for AAA accounting for this
event type.  The method is defined by the destination for
accounting data, which may be specified as the group of
all TACACS+/RADIUS servers, a defined server group, or
the local system.

args-id.name: args-id
args-id.description:
Current process command line arguments.  Arguments with
a parameter (e.g., --option 10  or -option=10) should be
represented as a single element of the list with the
argument name and parameter together.  Flag arguments, i.e.,
those without a parameter should also be in their own list
element.

authentication-method-id.name: authentication-method-id
authentication-method-id.description:
Ordered list of authentication methods for users.  This
can be either a reference to a server group, or a well-
defined designation in the AAA_METHOD_TYPE identity.  If
authentication fails with one method, the next defined
method is tried -- failure of all methods results in the
user being denied access.

authorization-method-id.name: authorization-method-id
authorization-method-id.description:
Ordered list of methods for authorizing commands.  The first
method that provides a response (positive or negative) should
be used.  The list may contain a well-defined method such
as the set of all TACACS or RADIUS servers, or the name of
a defined AAA server group.  The system must validate
that the named server group exists.

data_openconfig-system_system_aaa_authen.name: ldap
data_openconfig-system_system_aaa_authen.description: Top-level container for LDAP search settings.

data_openconfig-system_system_aaa_authen.name: ldap
data_openconfig-system_system_aaa_authen.description: Top-level container for LDAP search settings.

data_openconfig-system_system_aaa_authen.name: active_directory
data_openconfig-system_system_aaa_authen.description: Specifies whether this LDAP server is an Active Directory server (false by default).

data_openconfig-system_system_aaa_authen.name: base
data_openconfig-system_system_aaa_authen.description: The distinguished name (DN) to use as search base.

data_openconfig-system_system_aaa_authen.name: bind_timelimit
data_openconfig-system_system_aaa_authen.description:
The time limit (in seconds) to use when connecting to the directory server.
This is distinct from the time limit specified in timelimit and affects
the setup of the connection only.

data_openconfig-system_system_aaa_authen.name: binddn
data_openconfig-system_system_aaa_authen.description: The distinguished name with which to bind to the directory server for lookups.

data_openconfig-system_system_aaa_authen.name: bindpw
data_openconfig-system_system_aaa_authen.description: The credentials with which to bind when using binddn.

data_openconfig-system_system_aaa_authen.name: chase-referrals
data_openconfig-system_system_aaa_authen.description: Specifies whether automatic referral chasing should be enabled. The default behaviour is to chase referrals.

data_openconfig-system_system_aaa_authen.name: idle_timelimit
data_openconfig-system_system_aaa_authen.description:
The period of inactivity (in seconds) after which the connection to
the LDAP server will be closed. The default is not to time out connections.

data_openconfig-system_system_aaa_authen.name: ignore-case
data_openconfig-system_system_aaa_authen.description:
Specifies whether or not to perform searches for group, netgroup, passwd, protocols,
rpc, services and shadow maps using case-insensitive matching. By default, lookups
are case-sensitive.

data_openconfig-system_system_aaa_authen.name: ldap_version
data_openconfig-system_system_aaa_authen.description: Specifies the version number of the LDAP application. The default value is 3.

data_openconfig-system_system_aaa_authen.name: ssl
data_openconfig-system_system_aaa_authen.description: Specifies whether to use SSL/TLS (the default is not to use SSL/TLS).

data_openconfig-system_system_aaa_authen.name: timelimit
data_openconfig-system_system_aaa_authen.description:
Ihe time limit (in seconds) to wait for a response from the LDAP server.
A value of zero is to wait indefinitely for searches to be completed.

data_openconfig-system_system_aaa_authen.name: tls_cacert
data_openconfig-system_system_aaa_authen.description: Specifies the PEM-encoded X.509 certificate for peer authentication.

data_openconfig-system_system_aaa_authen.name: tls_cert
data_openconfig-system_system_aaa_authen.description: Specifies the PEM-encoded certificate for client TLS authentication.

data_openconfig-system_system_aaa_authen.name: tls_ciphers
data_openconfig-system_system_aaa_authen.description:
Specifies the ciphers to use for TLS. See your TLS implementation's documentation
for further information.

data_openconfig-system_system_aaa_authen.name: tls_key
data_openconfig-system_system_aaa_authen.description: Specifies the PEM-encoded private key for client TLS authentication.

data_openconfig-system_system_aaa_authen.name: tls_reqcert
data_openconfig-system_system_aaa_authen.description:
Specifies what checks to perform on server certificates in a TLS session.
The <level> can be specified as one of never, allow, try, demand, or hard.
A tls_cacert is required if peer verification is enabled.

which provide the uidNumber and gidNumber attributes from the directory. In a
generic AD environment without additional capabilities installed, these attributes

Ihe time limit (in seconds) to wait for a response from the LDAP server.
A value of zero is to wait indefinitely for searches to be completed. (leaf)

f5-openconfig-aaa-ldap:idle_timelimit.description:
The period of inactivity (in seconds) after which the connection to
the LDAP server will be closed. The default is not to time out connections. (leaf)

f5-openconfig-aaa-ldap:ldap_version.description: Specifies the version number of the LDAP application. The default value is 3. (leaf)

f5-openconfig-aaa-ldap:ssl.description: Specifies whether to use SSL/TLS (the default is not to use SSL/TLS). (leaf)

f5-openconfig-aaa-ldap:active_directory.description: Specifies whether this LDAP server is an Active Directory server (false by default). (leaf)

f5-openconfig-aaa-ldap:unix_attributes.description:
Specifies whether Unix Attributes have been enabled for Active Directory users,
which provide the uidNumber and gidNumber attributes from the directory. In a
generic AD environment without additional capabilities installed, these attributes
may need to be derived from the objectSid. This attribute has no effect for non-AD
servers. (leaf)

f5-openconfig-aaa-ldap:tls_reqcert.description:
Specifies what checks to perform on server certificates in a TLS session.
The <level> can be specified as one of never, allow, try, demand, or hard.
A tls_cacert is required if peer verification is enabled. (leaf)

f5-openconfig-aaa-ldap:tls_cacert.description: Specifies the PEM-encoded X.509 certificate for peer authentication. (leaf)

f5-openconfig-aaa-ldap:tls_ciphers.description:
Specifies the ciphers to use for TLS. See your TLS implementation's documentation
for further information. (leaf)

f5-openconfig-aaa-ldap:tls_cert.description: Specifies the PEM-encoded certificate for client TLS authentication. (leaf)

f5-openconfig-aaa-ldap:tls_key.description: Specifies the PEM-encoded private key for client TLS authentication. (leaf)

f5-openconfig-aaa-ldap:chase-referrals.description: Specifies whether automatic referral chasing should be enabled. The default behaviour is to chase referrals. (leaf)

f5-openconfig-aaa-ldap:ignore-case.description:
Specifies whether or not to perform searches for group, netgroup, passwd, protocols,
rpc, services and shadow maps using case-insensitive matching. By default, lookups
are case-sensitive. (leaf)

f5-openconfig-aaa-ldap:domain_sid.description: The root ObjectSID of the Active Directory domain. Only relevant for Active Directory configurations with unix_attributes false. (leaf)

f5-openconfig-aaa-ldap:binddn.description: The distinguished name with which to bind to the directory server for lookups. (leaf)

f5-openconfig-aaa-ldap:bindpw.description: The credentials with which to bind when using binddn. (leaf)

items.description: The distinguished name (DN) to use as search base. (leaf-list)

f5-openconfig-aaa-ldap:bind_timelimit.description:
The time limit (in seconds) to use when connecting to the directory server.
This is distinct from the time limit specified in timelimit and affects
the setup of the connection only. (leaf)

f5-openconfig-aaa-ldap:timelimit.description:
Ihe time limit (in seconds) to wait for a response from the LDAP server.
A value of zero is to wait indefinitely for searches to be completed. (leaf)
